channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chandra Kumar Bose joins TMC ahead of West Bengal Assembly elections, calls BJP move a mistake
item.description: In a significant political development ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections 2026, Chandra Kumar Bose, nephew of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose, formally joined the Trinamool Congress (TMC) on April...

item.title: Prime Minister Carney to hold bilateral talks with Finnish President Stubb in Ottawa
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is scheduled to meet with Finland’s President Alexander Stubb in Ottawa this week, marking the first official bilateral meeting between the two leaders. The discussions, pla...

item.title: Njugush and Celes tine Ndinda navigate separation rumours while pursuing individual goals
item.description: Drama has been brewing online regarding popular Kenyan couple Njugush and Celes tine Ndinda. Whispers of a separation started flying around after blogger Edgar O bare allegedly shared anonymous tips s...

item.title: Health risks linked to silver three-legged pots made from scrap metal
item.description: Researchers from the South African Medical Research Council have discovered that silver three-legged pots made from scrap metal could pose serious health risks. Dr Renee Street, director of environmen...

item.title: Manchester City aim to close gap on Arsenal as they face Chelsea in Premier League clash
item.description: Manchester City visit Stamford Bridge seeking to reduce Arsenal’s lead in the Premier League after the Gunners’ unexpected 2-1 home defeat to Bournemouth. Pep Guardiola’s team, buoyed by their recent ...

item.title: Canadians Question Accountability as MPs Switch Parties
item.description: Recent letters to the National Post reflect widespread frustration over Conservative MP Marilyn Gladu’s decision to cross the floor to the Liberal Party. Readers express concerns that such moves under...

item.title: Cowboy Chef Advocates for Phone-Free Family Dinners to Strengthen Bonds
item.description: Kent Rollins, a New Mexico-based cowboy chef known for his work on the Food Network and Outdoor Channel, is urging Americans to put down their phones during meals. Rollins emphasizes that the dinner t...

item.title: US and Iran fail to reach deal after Islamabad peace talks
item.description: US and Iran have ended their peace talks in Islamabad without reaching any deal, putting the fragile ceasefire at risk. The negotiations, which lasted over 21 hours, were the first direct high-level t...

item.title: School Assembly Headlines for April 13 Cover National, World, Sports, and Entertainment Updates
item.description: NDTV’s school assembly roundup for April 13 presents a concise selection of important developments from India and around the world. The article is designed for students and teachers who want a quick m...

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill Child During Funeral in Southern Lebanon
item.description: In southern Lebanon, tragedy struck the Saeed family as Israeli airstrikes targeted their village of Srifa on the first day of a US-Iran ceasefire, claiming lives despite hopes for peace. Seven-year-o...

item.title: The Role of Black Magic in Nigerian Culture and Music
item.description: Dis na one deep analysis wey look how black magic dey still influence life for Nigeria, even as modern religion and science dey spread. Festus Adedayo highlight how popular Yoruba musician, Saheed Osu...

item.title: DA's Cilliers Brink criticises ANC governance in Tshwane amid mayoral campaign
item.description: Cilliers Brink, the DA's mayoral candidate for Tshwane, has stated that his recent election as deputy federal chairperson of the party will not interfere with his campaign in the capital. Speaking to ...

item.title: US-Iran Talks in Islamabad End Without Agreement as Delegations Depart
item.description: The recent round of direct negotiations between the United States and Iran in Islamabad wrapped up on Sunday without a breakthrough. According to reports published by Dawn, discussions continued well ...

item.title: Modi Criticises TMC During West Bengal Poll Campaign, Highlights Regional Issues
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while campaigning for the BJP in West Bengal's Siliguri, strongly criticised the ruling Trinamool Congress (TMC), accusing it of promoting divisive elements from public s...

item.title: US Navy instructed to block Strait of Hormuz following failed negotiations with Iran
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has reportedly ordered the United States Navy to block the Strait of Hormuz after diplomatic talks with Iran did not yield a resolution. The move signals an escalation...

item.title: DA’s Brink addresses Tshwane governance concerns amid federal appointment
item.description: Cilliers Brink, the DA’s mayoral candidate for Tshwane, has reassured voters that his recent election as one of the party’s three deputy federal chairpersons will not interfere with his local campaign...

item.title: Australian Defence Force to Announce New Leadership Amid High-Profile Developments
item.description: Australia is set to see a historic shift in its military leadership as the Albanese government prepares to appoint new senior defence leaders. The announcement comes shortly after the arrest of decora...

item.title: Trump reports progress in Islamabad talks, says no breakthrough on Iran’s nuclear issue
item.description: In Islamabad, US President Donald Trump announced that his administration’s extended negotiations with Iran had made headway on several matters but ended without any understanding on the nuclear quest...

item.title: Karnataka Congress MLAs Press for Cabinet Reshuffle Including First-Time Legislators
item.description: A group of senior and first-time Indian National Congress MLAs from Karnataka has travelled to New Delhi to urge the party leadership for a cabinet reshuffle. First-time legislators are pressing for t...

item.title: Melburnian Belinda Trembath takes charge of Whistler, North America’s leading ski resort
item.description: Belinda 'BT' Trembath, a Melbourne-born ski instructor turned resort executive, is now at the helm of Whistler Blackcomb, often ranked North America’s premier ski destination. Having begun her career ...
